name: Frederick Stock
abstract: Frederick Stock (born Friedrich August Stock; November 11, 1872 – October 20, 1942) was a German and American conductor and composer, most famous for his 37-year tenure as music director of the Chicago Symphony Orchestra.

concerts.label: Mendelssohn Choir of Toronto with the Theodore Thomas Orchestra

works.t: 5 Gedichte für eine Frauenstimme (Wesendonck Lieder): 5. Träume

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: And then when they would take us down to Orchestra Hall, and Frederick Stock was the conductor, then, so this is -- that was a little of the background of the music.
